title: Game-improvement vs players irons: why forgiveness wins
microFact: Cavity-back irons forgive off-center hits; blades punish them. Even many pros now play cavity designs.
lesson:
Iron heads sit on a spectrum:

• SUPER GAME-IMPROVEMENT / GAME-IMPROVEMENT (GI): large heads, thick toplines, wide soles, and a hollowed-out back (CAVITY BACK) that pushes weight to the perimeter. Off-center strikes still fly far and straight-ish. This is your category — think Callaway Elyte X, TaylorMade Qi HL, Ping G440.
• PLAYERS DISTANCE: mid-size, for single-digit handicaps wanting some feel.
• BLADES (MUSCLE-BACK): slim, gorgeous, brutally honest. Miss the center by a groove and lose 15m.

The physics: perimeter weighting raises MOI (moment of inertia) — resistance to twisting when you strike off-center. High MOI = your mishits stay playable.

Social note, because it matters to you: NOBODY at an elite club judges GI irons — Ping G-series sits in half the bags at any members' club, including low handicappers. What gets silently judged is a 25-handicapper with blades. Forgiveness is what serious golfers choose; ego is what beginners choose.

quiz.question: What does high MOI in an iron head actually do?
options: Resists twisting on off-center hits, keeping mishits straighter
quiz.explanation: MOI is resistance to twisting. Perimeter-weighted heads twist less when you miss the sweet spot, so the ball flies straighter.
